perturbation.original_text: The Company represents and warrants to JANA that this Agreement has been duly authorized, executed and delivered by the Company, and is a valid and binding obligation of the Company, enforceable against the Company in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company represents and warrants to JANA that this Agreement has been duly authorized, executed and delivered by the Company, and is a valid and binding obligation of the Company, generally enforceable against the Company, but specific performance may not be required under certain circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces uncertainty by adding 'generally enforceable' and qualifying enforceability, which contradicts Section 11(a) that stipulates entitlement to seek specific performance. This introduces ambiguity as to whether the company is truly bound by the terms, because it also states that 'specific performance may not be required under certain circumstances'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Specific Performance; Remedies. (a) In furtherance and not in limitation of Section 11(b), the parties hereto shall be entitled to seek an injunction or injunctions to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement, in addition to any other remedy to which they are entitled at law or in equity. FURTHERMORE, EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO (A) IRREVOCABLY WAIVES THE RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY AND (B) AGREES TO WAIVE ANY BONDING REQUIREMENT UNDER ANY APPLICABLE LAW, IN THE CASE ANY OTHER PARTY SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE TERMS BY WAY OF EQUITABLE RELIEF. THIS AGREEMENT SHALL BE GOVERNED IN ALL RESPECTS, INCLUDING VALIDITY, INTERPRETATION AND EFFECT, BY THE LAWS OF THE STATE OF DELAWARE WITHOUT GIVING EFFECT TO THE CHOICE OF LAW PRINCIPLES OF SUCH STATE.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a significant ambiguity by stating that the agreement is 'generally enforceable' but that 'specific performance may not be required under certain circumstances.' This directly contradicts Section 11(a), which states that the parties 'shall be entitled to seek an injunction or injunctions...to enforce specifically the terms and provisions of this Agreement.' The original text provides an unqualified right to seek specific performance, while the changed text creates uncertainty about whether that right exists, thus creating a strong, clear in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Board shall appoint the New CEO as a director of the Company with a term expiring at the Company's next annual meeting after his or her appointment.
perturbation.changed_text: The Board shall aim to appoint the New CEO as a director of the Company with a term expiring at the Company's next annual meeting after his or her appointment, unless unforeseen circumstances prevent it.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a conditional element to the appointment of the New CEO as a director, by stating 'The Board shall aim to appoint' and adding the clause 'unless unforeseen circumstances prevent it'. This weakens the commitment to appoint, which contradicts the overall intention detailed in Section 4 (b) for CEO Succession.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CEO Succession. (a) At the March Board Meeting, the Company shall appoint two JANA Nominees chosen by JANA to the CEO Succession Committee of the Board (the "CEO Succession Committee") and shall appoint such JANA Nominees to any other committee currently or in the future designated to review or oversee the selection process for a successor to Mr. Koffel as chief executive officer or substantially similar position (the "New CEO"), and shall not otherwise increase the size of the CEO Succession Committee or any such other committee. In the event of the replacement as set forth in Section 3(c) of any JANA Nominee appointed to the CEO Succession Committee pursuant to this Section 5, his or her successor shall be promptly appointed to the committee seat vacated by such former director to serve until the 2015 Annual Meeting. The CEO Succession Committee and any other committee currently or in the future designated to review or oversee the selection process for the New CEO shall not take any material action prior to the March Board Meeting. (b) In order to ensure an orderly transition, Mr. Koffel may in his sole discretion continue (including, for the avoidance of doubt, after the 2014 Annual Meeting) as the Chief Executive Officer, Chairman of the Board and a director of the Company during the selection process for the New CEO until the Board shall appoint the New CEO, which shall not be later than the earlier of December 31, 2014, or Mr. Koffel's voluntary resignation from such positions; provided that Mr. Koffel will resign as a director of the Company and the Chairman of the Board upon the effectiveness of the Board's appointment of the New CEO; provided, further, that nothing contained herein is intended to modify any employment agreement, equity award, retirement plan or other pre-existing obligation of the Company to Martin Koffel or to impose any additional obligations on Martin Koffel (beyond the obligation to resign as provided in this Section 4(b)). The Board shall aim to appoint the New CEO as a director of the Company with a term expiring at the Company's next annual meeting after his or her appointment, unless unforeseen circumstances prevent it.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates that 'The Board shall appoint the New CEO as a director'. The changed text softens this obligation to 'The Board shall aim to appoint the New CEO as a director... unless unforeseen circumstances prevent it.' This directly contradicts the initial requirement for an 'orderly transition' outlined in Section 4(b), by introducing a condition that allows the board to avoid appointing the new CEO as director due to 'unforeseen circumstances'. The contradicted text requires the board to make this appointment, the changed text introduces an opt-out, creating a clear and impactful in-text contradiction. This introduction of conditionality creates an ambiguity on whether the appointment of the New CEO as director is compulsory. Therefore it also falls under the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
